channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rising rivers and flash floods threaten waterlogged US communities
item.description: DYERSBURG, Tennessee. (AP) — Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding came Saturday for parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that also spawned some deadly tornadoes. Day after day of heavy rains have pounded the central U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and OBED LAMY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Furious charter captain boards boat to berate young fisherman in viral video
item.description: A Florida charter boat captain has gone viral after he aggressively confronted a young fisherman over an alleged near-collision.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: St. Louis family's prank leads to lobster's prolonged life
item.description: What started off as an April Fool’s prank turned into something more than what Brea Stenzel, her family and a lobster were expecting.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, Fox 5

item.title: Man claims to be ICE agent, threatens to deport victims: Deputies
item.description: A Florida man is accused of pulling a vehicle over, claiming to be an ICE agent, and threatening to deport two victims.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Man claims to be ICE agent, threatens to deport victims, deputies say
item.description: A Florida man is accused of pulling a vehicle over, claiming to be an ICE agent, and threatening to deport two victims.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: NY public schools tell Trump administration they won't comply with DEI order
item.description: New York state school officials have told the Trump administration that they will not comply with its demands to end diversity, equity and inclusion practices, despite the administration’s threats to terminate federal education funding.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Is anything collectible worth collecting? What experts say
item.description: You may have been promised as a child that collecting plush toys and waxy baseball cards would pay off in the future.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's move to send Venezuelan migrants in the US to a prison in El Salvador
item.description: President Donald Trump has invoked a rarely used 1798 law that gives a president immense powers to arrest and deport noncitizens in a time of war.
item.dc:creator: Tim Sullivan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Takeaways from the Senate budget vote: Tariff pressure, debt worries and signs of GOP unease
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The political battle lines are drawn for a debate in Washington and beyond over a Republican budget plan that's a cornerstone of President Donald Trump's domestic agenda.
item.dc:creator: LEAH ASKARINAM and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Georgia bill would compensate the wrongfully convicted and let Trump recover costs of election case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A revived attempt to fix Georgia's inefficient system for compensating people wrongfully convicted of crimes almost died.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate vote-a-rama on Trump budget bill kicks off
item.description: The Senate on Friday night kicked off its vote-a-rama, a process that is likely to conclude with Republicans adopting the budget resolution that will serve as a blueprint for enacting President Trump's domestic agenda.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Sphere launching 'experience' around classic film
item.description: Sphere, the famed immersive Las Vegas entertainment arena, is set to debut two new "Sphere Experiences" later this year — and one is based on a beloved classic motion picture.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Army may slash 90,000 troops as Pentagon faces budget cuts and strategic shake-up: report
item.description: Massive potential cuts within the U.S. Army, reported by Military.com, would lead to a "leaner, more lethal, and larger" combat force, an Army spokesperson told Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actor Glen Powell launches organic condiments line
item.description: Actor and producer Glen Powell has launched Smash Kitchen, a line of organic condiments that includes non-GMO pantry essentials with remixed flavors, now exclusively available nationwide at Walmart stores and Walmart.com.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medicare won't cover anti-obesity GLP-1 drugs as Biden admin proposed
item.description: In November last year, the Biden administration proposed a new rule that would allow drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound to be covered by Medicare and Medicaid, expanding access for roughly 3.4 million Medicare users and about 4 million Medicaid enrollees.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration issues demands on Harvard as conditions for billions in federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has issued a list of demands Harvard University must meet as a condition for receiving almost $9 billion in grants and contracts, federal money that is being threatened during an investigation into campus antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disgraced former Cardinal Theodore McCarrick has died at age 94
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Theodore McCarrick, a once-powerful Catholic cardinal who was defrocked by Pope Francis in 2019 after a Vatican investigation determined he had molested adults and children, has died. He was 94.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dow plunges 2,000 as sell-off for markets slams into a scarier gear following Trump's tariffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The sell-off for financial markets worldwide is slamming into a higher, scarier gear on Friday. The S&P 500 tumbled 5.7% as Wall Street's worst crisis since the COVID crash deepened after China matched President Donald Trump’s big raise in tariffs announced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 100-year-old tortoise gives birth to critically endangered hatchlings at Philly Zoo
item.description: For the first time in over 150 years, the Philadelphia Zoo has new additions to the Western Santa Cruz Galapagos tortoise family.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas measles outbreak approaches 500 cases
item.description: The deadly measles outbreak in Texas is continuing to rise, now approaching a total of 500 confirmed cases with at least 56 people facing hospitalization since late January, according to health officials.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Trump's tariffs hit uninhabited islands filled with penguins
item.description: The implementation of tariffs on the islands did not go unnoticed by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls for Marine Le Pen to be freed
item.description: President Trump defended embattled French far-right leader Marine Le Pen, arguing there's a “witch hunt” against her.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — At least seven people have been killed in a wide swath of violent storms and tornadoes that hit the South and Midwest, and officials are bracing for more severe weather and flooding in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A man has shot and killed a Catholic priest at his parish rectory in northeast Kansas
item.description: SENECA, Kansas (AP) — A Catholic priest was shot and killed Thursday by a man who approached him at his parish rectory in the town of Seneca, Kansas, church officials said. “I am heartbroken to share the tragic news of the death of Fr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How the White House calculated reciprocal tariffs
item.description: President Trump triumphantly held up a poster board in the Rose Garden on Wednesday showing the reciprocal tariffs he plans to impose on nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Senate advances blueprint for Trump tax cuts, other top priorities
item.description: The Senate on Thursday advanced its budget resolution, teeing up a weekend vote on a blueprint for President Trump's tax agenda and other top priorities. Senators voted 52-48 to kick off consideration of the measure. A simple majority was needed.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No more cheap skirts: Trump ends tax exemption for low-value Chinese imports
item.description: A notice to customers dazzled by the low-priced products on Chinese shopping apps: the days of getting trendy clothing, tools and gag gifts that cost less than lunch delivered to your door in 10 days are probably numbered.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dow drops 1,600 as US stocks lead worldwide sell-off after Trump’s tariffs cause a COVID-like shock
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling Thursday following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe set of tariffs, and the U.S. stock market is taking the worst of it so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Yale fires legal scholar amid review of possible ties to 'sham charity' for designated terror group
item.description: Yale Law School has fired an Iranian scholar, accusing her of refusing to cooperate as it probed allegations that she is involved with a group that the U.S. calls a “sham charity” for a designated terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When heavy rainfall threatens flooding, you can protect yourself. Here's how
item.description: Extreme weather is dangerous weather, and that's particularly true for the heavy rainfall events that experts say are becoming more frequent with climate change. The powerful storms can pose threats ranging from falling limbs to downed power lines to drowning.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Outrage grows over Maryland man's mistaken deportation to El Salvador prison
item.description: In the 22 days since Maryland resident Kilmar Abrego Garcia was mistakenly deported to a notoriously violent prison in El Salvador, his young son has sought comfort in the scent of his missing father's clothes.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4
